talk_metadata.talk_title: The Genomic Landscape of Likely Human Pre-cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the vast majority of pre-cancers will never progress to cancer"
Speaker said: "we need better clinical data to be able to answer that specific question"

key_data_gaps: No longitudinal clinical outcome data for indolent pre‑cancers
No epigenetic, transcriptomic, immune or germline data included

crispro_opportunity.description: Need prospective biomarker‑driven validation to separate indolent from high‑risk pre‑cancers using the genomic risk score.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we would love to extend this to really ask the question that we want, which is: can we separate indolent precancers from high‑risk precancers? But we need better clinical data"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics (epigenomics, transcriptomics, immune profiling) with the genomic risk score to build a predictive model for progression risk.

unresolved_questions: How to capture clinical follow‑up for patients whose pre‑cancers regress or are lost to follow‑up?
What is the contribution of epigenetic and immune features to malignant potential?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Homologous recombination deficiency synthesise breast cancer to therapeutic targeting of ectonucleotidase ENPP1
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the combination, although it was a good idea, we don't see any effect in the patients over multiple clinical trials"
Speaker said: "we found this antibody ... was equal to the knocking out the gene entirely in tumors"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: ENPP1 blockade does not affect primary tumor growth without additional immune activation
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Only ~50% of mice showed tumor rejection after ENPP1 knockout

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Binding pocket is shallow; antibody shows similar efficacy to genetic knockout, suggesting limited chemical leverage
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Monotherapy with ENPP1 blockade shows minimal effect on primary tumor growth

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that ENPP1 knockout leads to tumor rejection in ~50% of mice, then concluded that ENPP1 is a promising single‑agent therapeutic target

key_data_gaps: No clinical efficacy data for ENPP1 antibody
Lack of biomarker‑stratified analysis in patients

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine ENPP1 inhibition with PARP inhibitor (niraparib) and anti‑PD‑1 to achieve synergistic tumor control.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "when we combined ENPP1 knockout with niraparib and anti‑PD‑1 we now started seeing really prolonged tumor control"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model the triple‑axis interaction (DNA repair, cGAMP signaling, checkpoint blockade) to prioritize optimal dosing schedules.

cited_competitors.name: Lee and Wells labs (Stanford/UCSF)
cited_competitors.context: Developed earlier ENPP1 inhibitors used as reference compounds

unresolved_questions: What is the mechanism by which ENPP1 loss enhances CD8 T‑cell infiltration?
Can ENPP1 inhibition overcome resistance to PARP inhibitors in BRCA‑deficient patients?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Mechanisms of Tumor Dormancy Through Immune Niche Formation and Its Impact on Immune Therapeutic Relapse
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we used inducible S2GP system ... the total lesion number was not dramatically changed early on, which is inconsistent with the cellular dormancy rather than failure of tumor seeding"
Speaker said: "we did not find any evidence for transdifferentiation"

key_data_gaps: No human validation of CD200‑mediated dormancy
Lack of therapeutic targeting data for CD200 in this context

crispro_opportunity.description: Target CD200‑CD200R axis to eliminate dormant lesions that evade NK‑cell killing.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "CD200 overexpression cells were less sensitive to NK cell killing"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can predict which dormant lesions express CD200 and simulate blockade effects on NK‑cell activity.

unresolved_questions: Does CD200 blockade synergize with existing checkpoint inhibitors in vivo?
What is the prevalence of CD200‑high dormant lesions in human breast cancer patients?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Michigan / Shanghai Institute of Chemistry
talk_metadata.talk_title: Ligandable PNT domain establishes ERG as a directable, targetable oncogenic driver in prostate cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "PBIDE1 is a weak but very selective"
Speaker said: "we have not yet assessed homology with other ETS family members beyond FLI1"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low binding affinity (28 µM) limits functional inhibition of ERG transcriptional program
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Only ERG‑positive cell lines showed growth inhibition; ERG‑negative lines were unaffected

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Binding pocket is shallow and formed by flexible loops, making high‑affinity optimization difficult
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on a single weak binder (PBIDE1) for preclinical efficacy

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated that PBIDE1 stabilizes ERG in thermal shift assays, yet concluded that the compound is sufficient to suppress tumor growth despite its weak affinity

key_data_gaps: Selectivity against other ETS family members not fully explored

crispro_opportunity.description: Improve affinity and drug‑likeness of PNT binders using CrisPRO‑guided fragment merging.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "PBIDE1 is a weak but very selective"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model the PNT pocket dynamics and propose scaffold extensions to increase binding energy.

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a diagnostic assay to identify ERG‑positive prostate cancers that would benefit from PNT‑targeted therapy.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "ERG is a stable and very reliable therapeutic target"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Integrate ERG IHC scores with CrisPRO predicted response to prioritize patients for trials.

cited_competitors.name: Parabilis Medicine
cited_competitors.context: Presented helicase degrader that also binds ERG PNT domain

unresolved_questions: Can higher‑affinity PNT binders achieve complete tumor regression in vivo?
What off‑target effects arise from binding to FLI1 PNT domain?

talk_metadata.talk_title: A novel brain‑penetrant BRAF inhibitor for BRAF mutant gliomas
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "HSK42360 demonstrated favorably with reported dabrafenib plus trametinib results"
Speaker said: "we did not observe any patients reach the response rate" (in resistant cohort)

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited drug exposure may underlie lack of responses in resistant cohorts
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "we did not observe any patients reach the response rate"

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on external rotation of αC helix; structural novelty may be circumvented by future RAF dimer‑stabilizing mutations
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Efficacy data limited to small glioma cohort; broader tumor type activity not yet proven

key_data_gaps: Limited pharmacokinetic exposure data linked to response

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a randomized phase II trial with a biomarker‑stratified arm to compare HSK42360 versus dabrafenib+trametinib in BRAF‑mutant glioma.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "HSK42360 demonstrated favorably with reported dabrafenib plus trametinib results"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial power calculations incorporating brain‑penetration PK/PD models to optimize enrollment criteria.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine HSK42360 with MEK inhibitor to further suppress MAPK reactivation in resistant tumors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "HSK42360 also shows antitumor activity ... both as monotherapy or in combination with a MEK inhibitor"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Predict synergistic dosing schedules using CrisPRO’s pathway modeling.

cited_competitors.name: Dabrafenib + Trametinib
cited_competitors.context: Standard of care comparator in glioma

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal exposure threshold for achieving responses in resistant patients?
Can HSK42360 overcome acquired RAF dimerization mutations?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Active RAS inhibition intercepts pancreas cancer in mice
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If you start and you eliminate pan‑en, preventing tumors from forming, you can actually extend overall survival by over a year"
Speaker said: "We have not done macrophage or fibroblast depletion experiments yet"

key_data_gaps: Translatability of mouse pan‑en interception to human high‑risk cohorts
Effect of stromal components (macrophages, fibroblasts) on interception efficacy

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply KRAS interception strategy to high‑risk human cohorts (e.g., germline predisposition, pancreatic cysts).
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "patients that we need to look at are the patients who are of highest risk for developing pancreas cancer"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model risk‑based screening intervals and predict interception benefit in defined populations.

crispro_opportunity.description: Combine KRAS inhibitors with agents targeting macrophages or fibroblasts to enhance interception.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we do see a decrease in macrophages with RAS inhibition, and that could potentially potentiate the effect"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate multi‑target perturbations in the tumor microenvironment to prioritize combos.

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal effective dosing schedule for long‑term interception in humans?
Can intermittent KRAS inhibition prevent emergence of resistant clones in pre‑malignant lesions?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Phase I/II trial of nab‑paclitaxel and gemcitabine with deruxnaratib in first‑line metastatic pancreatic cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We were unable to calculate median PFS and OS because there were not enough events yet"
Speaker said: "The combination demonstrated compelling initial evidence of activity"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: High incidence (73%) of grade ≥ 3 adverse events driven by overlapping rash and chemotherapy toxicities
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: Only 5% discontinued deruxnaratib due to toxicity, suggesting tolerability may improve with proactive management
vulnerability_identified.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker said: "the rash ... was low‑grade for most patients but 10‑15% had grade 3 or higher"

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad first‑line enrollment without biomarker‑driven stratification may dilute signal in KRAS‑wildtype patients
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: KRAS mutation status not required for entry (though most patients had KRAS mutations)
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Small sample (40 pts) limits robust efficacy estimates

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Shares binding site with other RAS inhibitors; potential for cross‑resistance
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Combination with standard chemotherapy adds cumulative toxicity, limiting dose intensity

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented high ORR (58%) yet acknowledged that median PFS/OS could not be calculated due to insufficient events, creating uncertainty about durability

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate a biomarker‑selected arm (KRAS G12D vs other) to enrich for patients most likely to benefit.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted 50% G12D, 25% G12V, 18% G12R distribution and suggested allele‑specific considerations
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate response rates by KRAS allele to power a stratified trial design.

crispro_opportunity.description: Evaluate deruxnaratib with less toxic chemotherapy backbones (e.g., nab‑paclitaxel alone) or with immune checkpoint inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed rash management and the desire to reduce chemotherapy exposure
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model synergistic MAPK inhibition with immune activation to identify lower‑toxicity combos.

cited_competitors.name: Dabrafenib + Trametinib
cited_competitors.context: Referenced as comparator for BRAF inhibitor efficacy

unresolved_questions: Will allele‑specific RAS inhibitors (e.g., G12D‑selective) outperform multi‑selective deruxnaratib in this setting?
What is the optimal duration of deruxnaratib therapy to balance efficacy and rash toxicity?
